name: Anthracnose
description: Brown to black sunken lesions on leaves, stems, and fruit. Spots often have concentric rings with yellow halos. Caused by Colletotrichum spp. High humidity promotes spread.
recommendation: Apply copper-based fungicides (Copper oxychloride 0.3%). Remove infected plant parts. Improve air circulation through pruning. Spray every 7-14 days in wet season.

name: Canker
description: Dark water-soaked lesions on stems and branches that become sunken and cracked. Gum exudation from cankers. Bark splits and peels. Branch dieback in severe cases.
recommendation: Scrape infected bark and apply copper paste. Inject streptomycin for bacterial canker. Prune affected branches 15-20 cm below symptoms. Disinfect tools between cuts.

name: Fruit Rot
description: Water-soaked spots on fruit that rapidly enlarge into soft brown decay. White, gray, or green mold growth. Foul odor. Premature fruit drop. Internal flesh becomes mushy.
recommendation: Remove infected fruits from tree and ground. Apply Mancozeb or Metalaxyl. Bag developing fruits. Improve orchard sanitation. Reduce humidity through pruning.

name: Mealybug
description: White cottony masses on leaves, stems, and young shoots. Stunted growth and leaf yellowing. Honeydew secretion leads to sooty mold. Ants farm mealybugs for honeydew.
recommendation: Apply systemic insecticides (Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam) or biological control (Cryptolaemus ladybird beetles). Use neem oil. Control ant populations. Prune heavily infested parts.

name: Pink Disease
description: Pinkish-white cobweb-like fungal growth on stems and branches. Bark cracks and peels. Branch dieback from tip downward. Caused by Corticium salmonicolor.
recommendation: Prune infected branches 30-50 cm below visible signs. Apply fungicide paste (bitertanol, propiconazole). Improve air circulation. Avoid dense planting.

name: Sooty Mold
description: Black soot-like coating on upper leaf surfaces. Fungus grows on honeydew from sucking insects. Reduced photosynthesis. Leaves may yellow and drop.
recommendation: Control underlying insect pests. Wash leaves with strong water spray or mild soap. Apply copper-based fungicides. Introduce beneficial insects (ladybirds, lacewings).

name: Stem Blight
description: Dark elongated lesions on stems and branches. Rapid wilting above infection. Bark discoloration and cracking. Vascular discoloration under bark. Girdling leads to branch death.
recommendation: Prune infected branches 20-30 cm below symptoms. Apply fungicide paste to wounds. Spray Triadimefon or Propiconazole. Improve drainage. Disinfect pruning tools.

name: Stem Cracking Gummosis
description: Longitudinal cracks on stems and branches with amber to brown gum exudation. Bark separates from wood. Secondary infections common in cracked areas.
recommendation: Improve drainage. Apply wound dressing (Bordeaux paste) to cracks. Reduce irrigation in wet season. Apply calcium and boron supplements. Prevent mechanical injury.

name: Thrips
description: Silvery-white streaks and stippling on young leaves. Leaves become distorted, curled, and stunted. Brown scabby patches on leaf undersides. Black fecal spots visible.
recommendation: Apply Spinosad, Abamectin, or Imidacloprid. Use yellow sticky traps. Apply neem oil. Introduce predatory mites (Amblyseius). Spray both upper and lower leaf surfaces.

name: Yellow Leaf
description: Uniform or interveinal yellowing of leaves. Older leaves yellow first (N deficiency). Interveinal chlorosis with green veins (Fe/Mg deficiency). Leaf tips and margins may brown.
recommendation: Apply balanced NPK fertilizer based on soil test. Foliar spray with micronutrients (Fe, Mg, Zn, Mn). Correct soil pH to 5.5-6.5. Improve drainage. Add organic matter.

name: Healthy
description: No disease detected. Tree is in healthy condition with no visible symptoms.
recommendation: Continue regular monitoring and preventive care. Maintain good orchard hygiene and irrigation practices.
